title: Split blender_server.py Handlers by Domain
description: blender_server.py is 1400+ lines with all command handlers in a single file. Split handlers into domain-specific modules mirroring the tools/commands/ client-side structure. Also identify client-only operations (e.g. promote) that receive a BlenderClient parameter they never use, and make the registry support client-only commands without requiring a running Blender instance.
definition-of-done: 1. Server handlers split into separate modules (geometry_handlers, node_handlers, lifecycle_handlers, etc.). 2. blender_server.py imports and registers handlers from submodules. 3. COMMAND_HANDLERS dict is auto-assembled from submodules. 4. Client-only CLI commands (promote) skip the BlenderClient requirement. 5. All existing CLI commands still work.
testing-plan: 1. Run blender-cli ping to verify server startup. 2. Run blender-cli validate --group NeckArmor to verify full pipeline. 3. Run blender-cli promote --group test --dest ... to verify client-only path.
todos: TODO : Create procedural_human/testing/handlers/ package with domain modules
TODO : Move geometry handlers (validate_geometry, check_watertight, etc.) to handlers/geometry.py
TODO : Move node handlers (export, inspect, list_groups, check_node_tree) to handlers/nodes.py
TODO : Move lifecycle handlers (reload, open_file, clean_scene) to handlers/lifecycle.py
TODO : Auto-assemble COMMAND_HANDLERS from submodule registrations
TODO : Add client-only command support to cli_registry (skip client param injection)
unknowns: Should handlers use a decorator for self-registration (like @cli_command) or explicit dicts?
How should the handler modules handle shared state (e.g. _log, _log_path)?
